# SL-INDEX-006: noindex-rendered-only

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

A noindex directive appears only after JavaScript rendering.

## Metadata

- Category: Indexability (indexability)
- Default severity: error
- Confidence: likely
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- raw-html
- rendered-dom

## Required Evidence

- raw-directives
- rendered-directives

## When This Triggers

Compare robots directives in raw HTML and rendered DOM.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Emit indexability directives consistently in server-rendered HTML.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/indexability/noindex-rendered-only

Fixed direction:

- Emit indexability directives consistently in server-rendered HTML.

selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.
